stories.headline: US-Iran War: Military Operations, Diplomacy, and Regional Impact

articles.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
articles.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

articles.title: How passenger planes keep flying during a war
articles.description: Airspace over Iran and the Gulf remains severely restricted following missile strikes, putting additional pressure on airlines.

articles.title: How the U.S. is using AI in the war in Iran
articles.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Lauren Kahn of Georgetown University's Center for Security and Emerging Technology about the role of artificial intelligence in war.

articles.title: 'We never asked for a ceasefire,' says Iran's foreign minister, as war keeps raging
articles.description: Israel announced a barrage of new strikes on western Iran on Sunday, while Iran's foreign minister said the country has not asked for a ceasefire as President Trump had claimed.

articles.title: Iran's foreign minister rejects talk of dialogue with U.S. as strikes continue
articles.description: The war, which shows no signs of ending soon, has upended global air travel, disrupted oil exports from the region and sent fuel prices rising across the world.

articles.title: Lasers may be the next frontier for stopping Iran's cheap drones
articles.description: Taking down Iran's cheap drones is a drain on the U.S. weapons stockpile. Emerging laser technology may be a cost-effective key to taking them down.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz disruption threatens to shake global economy
articles.description: The strategic shipping lane carries a fifth of the world's oil. Analysts warn prolonged disruption could send fuel prices soaring and trigger a global economic slowdown.

articles.title: U.S. searches for answers to Iran's arsenal of cheap drones
articles.description: The Iran war is testing the U.S. military's ability to combat Iran's drones in a cost-effective way. Emerging laser tech, designed to zap drones out of the sky, may help.

articles.title: Not just gas: Grocery prices could be hit by Strait of Hormuz closure
articles.description: Iranian attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz threaten to drive U.S. gas prices to record highs. The attacks could also have a cascading effect on other prices.

articles.title: Entering War’s Third Week, Trump Faces Stark Choices
articles.description: As the conflict with Iran expands and intensifies, President Trump’s options — to fight on, or to move toward declaring victory and pulling back — both carry deeply problematic consequences.

articles.title: Trump calls for other nations to help reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: President Trump’s call for help to reopen the Strait of Hormuz has so far gone unanswered. On Truth Social, the president listed China, France, Japan, South Korea, the U.K. as countries he hopes would help. It comes as Trump tells NBC News that Iran is ready to negotiate a ceasefire. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez has the latest.

articles.title: Ukraine's anti-drone tech is in high demand as Iran attacks its neighbors
articles.description: KYIV, Ukraine — As the conflict in the Middle East escalates, Ukraine could prove to be an invaluable trove of battle-tested expertise from its own bitter and costly fight against Russia

articles.title: Trump says Iran is ready to negotiate a ceasefire but he's not ready to make a deal
articles.description: In a wide-ranging interview, President Donald Trump said he’s not ready to make a deal to end the war with Iran, despite Iran’s willingness to do so.

articles.title: Trump says there’s been ‘total obliteration’ of Iran militarily
articles.description: President Trump told NBC News’ Kristen Welker in a nearly half hour phone conversation that Iran has been “militarily defeated” and he believes other countries will help to secure passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Sec. Wright says Iran war could be over in '4 to 6 weeks'
articles.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright answers Kristen Welker on how long he anticipates the war in Iran to continue.

articles.title: Trump says military targets on Iranian island 'obliterated'
articles.description: Trump says military targets on Iranian island 'obliterated'

articles.title: Why gas prices have risen despite record U.S. oil production
articles.description: Why have gas prices been rising since the start of war in Iran if U.S. oil production is at record highs? NBC News’ Christine Romans explains.

articles.title: Chris Wright addresses concerns over surging energy prices
articles.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright addresses concerns over surging energy prices as the war with Iran triggers the largest oil supply disruption in history.

articles.title: Energy secretary: Strait of Hormuz not safe for shipping
articles.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright tells Kristen Welker on “Meet the Press” that the Strait of Hormuz is not currently safe for commercial shipping, but the administration is working with other countries to secure the passageway.

articles.title: Video shows U.S. strikes on Iran's Kharg Island
articles.description: Video shows U.S. strikes on Iran's Kharg Island

articles.title: Iran war live: Tehran rejects Trump claim on talks; fire near Dubai airport
articles.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Tehran never sought a ceasefire with the US, and is ready for a long war.

articles.title: Top Trump adviser says Iran war price tag at $12bn so far
articles.description: Pressure grows on the US president's administration as war costs spiral and the mission's endgame remains unclear.

articles.title: Muted response as Trump urges nations to escort ships through Hormuz Strait
articles.description: The US president's call for allied naval forces comes as Iran's Revolutionary Guard says, 'Let him send his ships'.

articles.title: What’s the impact of the Iran war on China?
articles.description: The US-Israel war on Iran rages on and China says it should never have happened.

articles.title: Iran’s ambassador to Saudi Arabia denies attacks on its oil facilities
articles.description: Alireza Enayati says relations with Saudi Arabia are 'progressing naturally' and he's in contact with Saudi officials.

articles.title: What is force majeure and why are some Gulf countries invoking it?
articles.description: Several Gulf energy producers have declared force majeure on oil and gas shipments after disruptions to shipping.

articles.title: Different parties ‘on the rise’ within the anti-Netanyahu right
articles.description: Political analyst Dahlia Scheindlin explains why Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's popularity is fluctuating.

articles.title: US Senator Van Hollen: Trump ‘has no idea where this war is going’
articles.description: Democratic US Senator Chris Van Hollen argues that US-Israel war on Iran ‘is making America and our allies less safe'.

articles.title: IDF says it’s planning for at least 3 more weeks of Iran operation
articles.description: Israel Defense Forces (IDF) on Sunday said it’s planning to continue strikes on Iran for at least three more weeks. “We have thousands of targets ahead,” IDF spokesperson Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin told CNN.  “We are ready, in coordination with our U.S. allies, with plans through at least the Jewish holiday of Passover, about three…

articles.title: Trump raises Democratic attacks by downplaying high fuel prices sparked by Iran war
articles.description: President Trump is downplaying concerns about rising gas and oil prices that have been badly exacerbated by the war in Iran. Both the president and members of his Cabinet have described rising prices as a “small price to pay” to achieve their goals in Iran. Trump also argued that rising prices are good for the…

articles.title: Hassett says US has ‘got what we need’ on funding for Iran conflict
articles.description: National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett on Sunday said the Trump administration has “got what we need” when it comes to funding for the conflict in Iran. “Well, tell me how much is this war going to cost? Because I know the Pentagon briefed it’s going to be about $11.3 billion. Do you need to…

articles.title: Senate Democrat: Congress ‘feckless’ in handing over war powers
articles.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) on Sunday said Congress has been “feckless” in allowing President Trump’s authority to expand and cited the war with Iran as an example of Congress failing to check the executive branch’s growing power. “Well, listen, I’m going to be one of those Democrats that say I think both parties have been…

articles.title: Pence on Iran conflict: ‘It’s about finishing a war that’s nearly 50 years in the making’
articles.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence on Sunday said President Trump’s strikes on Iran are “finishing” a decades-long war with leaders in Tehran. “One administration after another has slashed at the tentacles of Iran that have taken violence to American forces, to American citizens, to our allies in the region, most especially Israel, and making that…

articles.title: Warner on deadly Iranian girls’ school strike: ‘I want a thorough investigation’
articles.description: Sen. Mark Warner (D-Va.) on Sunday discussed a recent strike on an Iranian girls’ school, saying he desires “a thorough investigation” into the matter. “We’ve only got preliminary assessments, and I want a thorough investigation. But what I don’t want to do is jump to the conclusion, whether it was [U.S. Central Command (Centcom)] or…

articles.title: Senate Republican rebukes Carr’s threats to revoke broadcast licenses over Iran coverage
articles.description: Sen. Ron Johnson (R-Wis.) on Sunday rebuked Federal Communications Commission (FCC) Chair Brendan Carr’s threats to revoke broadcasters’ licenses over TV networks’ news coverage. “I am a big supporter of the First Amendment,” Johnson said on Fox News’s “The Sunday Briefing.” “I do not like the heavy-handed government, no matter who is wielding it. … I would rather…

articles.title: Energy secretary on gas prices: ‘Americans will feel it for a few more weeks’
articles.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright said Sunday that Americans can expect to experience high prices at the gas pump “for a few more weeks” amid the U.S. conflict in Iran. “Yes, it is a short-term disruption in the flow of energy. Americans are feeling it right now. Americans will feel it for a few more weeks,…

articles.title: Energy secretary: US conflict in Iran will ‘certainly’ end in ‘next few weeks’
articles.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright said Sunday that he thinks the U.S. conflict in Iran could end in the next few weeks — or even sooner. “I think that this conflict will certainly come to an end in the next few weeks,” Wright told ABC News’s Martha Raddatz on “This Week.” “Could be sooner than that,…

articles.title: Ted Cruz, Tucker Carlson reignite feud over Iran war
articles.description: Cruz called Carlson a “demagogue” spreading antisemitism, days after Carlson called him and others who trust Israeli intelligence “dumbos.”

articles.title: U.S. stock futures bounce back, oil falls as investors weigh developments in Iran conflict
articles.description: U.S. stock-index futures reversed early losses on Sunday as the market braced for another surge in oil prices this week, with the conflict with Iran threatening to escalate further.

articles.title: It was unthinkable a couple of weeks ago, but could the next move by the Fed be a rate hike?
articles.description: With fuel prices soaring since the start of the war with Iran, a question has surfaced that was almost unimaginable a few weeks ago.

articles.title: The financial sector is sending some spooky technical signals about the stock market
articles.description: Broken trendlines and a looming ‘death cross’ pattern bode badly for the financial sector, and therefore the rest of the stock market.

articles.title: ‘It’s all about oil’: FedEx’s transportation costs will be key to this week’s earnings
articles.description: With results due this week from FedEx and retail names like Lululemon and Macy’s, we’ll likely hear more about consumers’ attitudes, as gas prices and shipping costs spike and the conflict widens in the Middle East.

articles.title: Individual investors are chasing oil’s surge amid Iran conflict; institutions are thinking about what comes next
articles.description: A popular oil fund pulled in $1 billion from investors in 9 days, while an ETF that tracks the S&P 500 shed $12.6 billion

articles.title: Panic is slowly gripping the stock market. Expect the selling to pick up this week.
articles.description: Options traders are signaling trouble, and systematic funds are expected to cut their exposure to U.S. stocks.

articles.title: CNN liberal panelist argues billions spent on defense should go to health insurance instead
articles.description: CNN panelists debate U.S. priorities as Leigh McGowan questions whether spending on the Iran conflict should instead go toward addressing healthcare challenges at home.

articles.title: Iranian foreign minister claims Trump launched war 'because it is fun'
articles.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Aragchi accused President Donald Trump of launching attacks against his country "for fun" during a "Face the Nation" interview.

articles.title: Trump talks with Starmer as he tries to lure countries to help reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: President Donald Trump spoke with U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer by phone on Sunday, a conversation that touched on Iran‘s chokehold of the Strait of Hormuz. According to a statement from Starmer’s office, the two discussed the Iran war and the “importance of reopening the Strait of Hormuz to end the disruption to global shipping.” […]

articles.title: Araghchi says Iran never asked to reopen talks with the US: ‘Don’t see any reason’
articles.description: Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Sunday that Iran “never asked” for a ceasefire in the ongoing conflict with Israel and the United States. Araghchi denied President Donald Trump’s claim that Iran wanted a deal to end the conflict, but he rejected it. The foreign minister accused Trump of engaging in this conflict because he […]

articles.title: IRGC leaders try to calm fears after Israeli strikes target Basij checkpoints
articles.description: Leaders of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps sought to reassure the public after Israeli strikes targeting Basij checkpoints and security facilities across Iran, raising concerns about the regime’s ability to maintain internal control during the widening conflict.  Iranian state media and IRGC officials said security forces remain in control following the strikes, which reportedly hit […]

articles.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards pledge to kill Netanyahu
articles.description: Iranian leadership on Sunday threatened to kill Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu as the war in the region continues to escalate.  The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said via Iranian state media that it “vows to pursue and kill ‘child-killer’ Netanyahu if he is still alive,” while simultaneously suggesting — without evidence — that the Israeli leader […]

articles.title: Trump Reverses Obama on Iran
articles.description: There’s no doubt that Trump’s vision of the Middle East is more in keeping with U.S. interests than Obama’s.

articles.description: Two of the plan’s proposals, when taken together, show the Trump administration’s hostility to free trade, as well as a general naïveté.

analysis.summary: The US-Iran war has entered its third week with ongoing US and Israeli strikes on Iranian targets, including military installations on Kharg Island. Iran's foreign minister has denied Trump's claims that Tehran sought a ceasefire, while the Strait of Hormuz remains unsafe for commercial shipping, driving oil prices to $100 per barrel and causing widespread economic disruption. Trump has called on allied nations to help reopen the strait, but international response has been muted.
agreements: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi publicly denied that Iran asked for a ceasefire, contradicting President Trump's claims
The Strait of Hormuz is currently disrupted and unsafe for commercial shipping
Trump called on multiple nations to join a coalition to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz
The international response to Trump's call for help in the Strait of Hormuz has been limited or muted so far
The war has driven oil prices sharply higher and raised fuel prices for consumers
US forces struck military targets on Iran's Kharg Island
Energy Secretary Chris Wright predicted the conflict could end within weeks
The war is in its third week with no clear end in sight
Israel is conducting strikes on targets inside Iran in coordination with the US
Iran's cheap drone arsenal poses a significant cost and capability challenge for US forces

disagreements.topic: How the war is characterized and who is involved

positions.position: Consistently frames the conflict as the 'US-Israel war on Iran,' emphasizing Israel's co-belligerent role

positions.source: Most US sources (NBC, CBS, The Hill, Fox News, etc.)
positions.position: Frame the conflict primarily as the 'US war with Iran' or 'Iran war,' with Israel mentioned as a participating ally rather than a co-equal combatant

disagreements.topic: Whether Iran is willing to negotiate

positions.position: Trump stated Iran is ready to negotiate a ceasefire but he is not ready to make a deal

positions.source: NPR / Al Jazeera / CBS News / Washington Examiner
positions.position: Iran's FM Araghchi says Iran never asked for a ceasefire and sees no reason for talks with the US

disagreements.topic: Cost of the war so far

positions.position: Reports a top Trump adviser put the war's price tag at approximately $12 billion
Reports the Pentagon briefed the cost at about $11.3 billion

disagreements.topic: US military success and Iran's stability

positions.position: Trump claims 'total obliteration' of Iran militarily and a 'dominant victory'; administration officials echo this framing
US intelligence assesses the Iranian government is not at risk of collapse
Frames Trump as facing 'stark choices' with deeply problematic consequences regardless of whether he fights on or pulls back

disagreements.topic: Expected timeline for the war's end

positions.position: Energy Secretary Wright estimated 4 to 6 weeks, possibly sooner
IDF spokesperson said they are planning for at least 3 more weeks with 'thousands of targets ahead'
Emphasize the war shows no signs of ending soon and the endgame remains unclear

disagreements.topic: Emphasis on economic consequences vs. military progress

positions.position: Heavily emphasize economic fallout: oil at $100/barrel, stock market sell-offs, potential Fed rate hikes, consumer price impacts
Focus more on political debates, administration messaging, and domestic support for the war effort, with economic concerns treated as secondary

disagreements.topic: Whether rising fuel prices are a serious concern

positions.position: Reports Trump and Cabinet members describe rising prices as a 'small price to pay,' drawing Democratic attacks
Frame the price increases as a major threat to the global economy and American consumers

uniqueAngles.angle: Iranian state media coverage includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran

Tehran claims the US launched attacks from UAE territory
Turkey's Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan discussed diplomatic efforts as the regional war continues
Trump demanded 'about 7' countries join a coalition to police the Strait of Hormuz
Several Gulf energy producers have declared force majeure on oil and gas shipments due to shipping disruptions
Iran's ambassador to Saudi Arabia denied attacks on Saudi oil facilities and said relations with Saudi Arabia are 'progressing naturally'
Ukraine's battle-tested anti-drone technology and expertise is in high demand as Iran attacks its neighbors
Trump listed China, France, Japan, South Korea, and the UK as countries he hopes would help reopen the Strait of Hormuz
IDF spokesperson Brig. Gen. Effie Defrin said Israel has plans through at least the Jewish holiday of Passover, about three more weeks, with 'thousands of targets ahead'
Sen. Mark Warner called for a thorough investigation into a strike on an Iranian girls' school
Sen. Ron Johnson rebuked FCC Chair Brendan Carr's threats to revoke broadcast licenses over TV networks' Iran war coverage
Sen. Cory Booker called Congress 'feckless' in handing over war powers to the executive branch
Ted Cruz and Tucker Carlson reignited a public feud over the Iran war, with Cruz calling Carlson a 'demagogue' and Carlson calling Cruz and others who trust Israeli intelligence 'dumbos'

The IRGC pledged via Iranian state media to pursue and kill Netanyahu

Iranian attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz could have cascading effects on grocery prices, not just gasoline

analysis.summary: The 98th Academy Awards, held on March 15, 2026, at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles and hosted by Conan O'Brien, featured historic moments including a rare tie for Best Live-Action Short Film and Sean Penn winning Best Supporting Actor but not attending the ceremony. analysis.summary: Conan O'Brien hosted the 97th/98th Academy Awards, delivering an opening monologue that mixed comedy with political references and a serious tone. He took apparent aim at Donald Trump without naming him and spoke about the current times being 'chaotic' and 'frightening,' while also expressing optimism about the ceremony as an international event.
agreements: Conan O'Brien hosted the Oscars and delivered a politically charged opening monologue
The monologue included political references and commentary on current events

disagreements.topic: Framing of O'Brien's monologue tone and intent

positions.position: Frames the monologue primarily as O'Brien taking political jabs, specifically at Donald Trump and MAGA
Frames the monologue as striking a 'serious' note about chaotic times while also offering optimism, with less emphasis on direct political attacks

uniqueAngles.angle: O'Brien did not mention Donald Trump by name when making his jab, and the reference came early in the ceremony
O'Brien specifically called the Oscars 'an international event' and described the current period as 'very chaotic, frightening times,' framing the ceremony as a source of optimism

stories.headline: State Department Cuts Fee to Renounce US Citizenship to $450

articles.title: State Department Cuts Price of Renouncing U.S. Citizenship to $450
articles.description: The fee had been increased to $2,350 in 2015, prompting criticism and legal challenges from advocacy groups and Americans living abroad.

articles.title: State Department slashes fee to renounce US citizenship by 80% to $450 - AP News
articles.description: State Department slashes fee to renounce US citizenship by 80% to $450  AP News

analysis.summary: The U.S. State Department has reduced the fee to renounce American citizenship from $2,350 to $450, representing an approximately 80% decrease from the price set in 2015.
agreements: The new fee to renounce U.S. citizenship is $450
The previous fee was $2,350
The State Department implemented the fee reduction

uniqueAngles.angle: The 2015 fee increase had prompted criticism and legal challenges from advocacy groups and Americans living abroad
Characterized the reduction as an 80% cut in its headline

stories.headline: Fox News and Dominion Voting Systems Reach Defamation Settlement

articles.title: Some on-air claims about Dominion Voting Systems were false, Fox News acknowledges in statement after deal is announced
Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
Here are the 20 specific Fox broadcasts and tweets Dominion says were defamatory
articles.description:
• Fox-Dominion trial delay 'is not unusual,' judge says
• Fox News' defamation battle isn't stopping Trump's election lies

articles.title: Judge in Fox News-Dominion defamation trial: 'The parties have resolved their case'
articles.description: The judge just announced in court that a settlement has been reached in the historic defamation case between Fox News and Dominion Voting Systems.

articles.title: 'Difficult to say with a straight face': Tapper reacts to Fox News' statement on settlement
articles.description: A settlement has been reached in Dominion Voting Systems' defamation case against Fox News, the judge for the case announced. The network will pay more than $787 million to Dominion, a lawyer for the company said.
